revision.text:
[[ja:アイセルシュラホール]] {{Building |Name = Fujidera Municipal Lifelong Learning Center
Aiseru Shura Hall |Former Name = |image = [[File:Aicelshurahall01 1024.jpg|300px]] |Purpose = Museum and Lifelong Learning Center |Former Purpose = |Architect = {{ill|Rui Design Office|ja|類設計室|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rui Design Office|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rui Design Office|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rui Design Office|1=Rui Design Office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11665177}} |Structural Engineer = |Constructor = |Owner = Fujidera City |Operator = |Structural Type = SRC, RC, S, 4 floors above ground, 1 basement, retractable roof |Site Area = 14,129m² |Building Area = |Total Floor Area = 4,759m² |Floors = |Height = |Start Date = |Opening Date = |Renovation Date = |Cultural Property Designation = |Designation Date = |Postal Code = 583-0024 |address = {{JPN|qq=}} Osaka Prefecture, Fujidera City, Fujidera 3-1-20 |Latitude degrees = 34 |Latitude minutes = 33 |Latitude seconds = 58.03 |Latitude N or S = N |Longitude degrees = 135 |Longitude minutes = 35 |Longitude seconds = 50.03 |Longitude E or W = E |Map Country Code = JP |Display Coordinates = 1 |Map Type = Japan Osaka }} [[File:Aicelshurahall02 1024.jpg|thumb|Southwest direction]] [[File:Aicelshurahall04 1024.jpg|thumb|160px|West side]] '''AICEL Shura Hall''' is the nickname for a lifelong learning center in {{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|qid=Q634011|lt=Fujidera City|1=Fujiidera}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan, which primarily serves as an {{ill|Archaeology|en|Archaeology|qid=Q23498|lt=Archaeology|1=Archaeology}} and {{ill|History|en|History|qid=Q309|lt=History|1=History}} museum. Its official name is '''Fujidera Municipal Lifelong Learning Center'''. The facility opened in {{ill|1994|en|1994|qid=Q2067|lt=1994|1=1994}}. The name "AICEL" is derived from the initials of '''A'''ctivity, '''I'''nformation, '''C'''onsultation, '''E'''xchange, and '''L'''earning.[https://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/sekaiisan/siru/foreignlanguage/languages/english/fujiiderasightseeingfacilityinformation/shurahall.html AICEL-Shura Hall／藤井寺市][https://howtojapan.net/2022/08/01/fujiidera-city-%e8%97%a4%e4%ba%95%e5%af%ba%e5%b8%82-my-city-became-a-world-heritage-site-finally/ Fujiidera City 藤井寺市: My city became a world heritage site finally? -] "Shura" refers to the giant stone-moving sleds ("{{ill|Shura (sled)|ja|修羅 (そり)|lt=Shura|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shura (sled)|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shura (sled)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shura (sled)|qq=}}"") excavated from {{ill|Mitsuzuka Kofun|ja|三ツ塚古墳|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuzuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuzuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuzuka Kofun|qq=}} during the [[Kofun period]][https://web.archive.org/web/20170802081310/https://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/shisetsuannaibasu/kyoikubunka/1387002133055.html Aiseru Shura Hall (Fujidera Municipal Lifelong Learning Center)｜Fujidera City] (Accessed June 24, 2018).. The building's exterior design is inspired by the boat-shaped {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} unearthed from {{ill|Oka Kofun|ja|岡古墳|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oka Kofun|qq=}}[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/kanko/spot/kankospotall/1473301496864.html Fujidera Municipal Lifelong Learning Center "Aiseru Shura Hall" 2nd Floor History Exhibition Zone｜Fujidera City] (Accessed June 24, 2018).. The building is located immediately to the west of '''{{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamisanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamisanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|13=User:Immanuelle/User:Immanuelle/Okamisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamisanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamisanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamisanzai Kofun|qq=}}'''[https://ok-habikino.jp/en/spot/88/ AICEL-Shura Hall | [Official] Osaka Habikino Tourism Bureau] the grave of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|qid=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}}.[https://ok-habikino.jp/en/spot/85/ Chuai-tenno-ryo Kofun | [Official] Osaka Habikino Tourism Bureau] ==Facilities== * History Exhibition Room ** Themed displays on "The Five Kings of Wa and Giant [[Kofun]]s," showcasing kofun construction techniques and excavated artifacts from {{ill|Nishihayama Kofun|ja|西墓山古墳|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishihayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishihayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishihayama Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Tsudo Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳|1=Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsudo Shiroyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|13=User:Immanuelle/User:Immanuelle/Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsudo Shiroyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsudo Shiroyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun|qq=}} ** Exhibits titled "Ancient Fujidera," introducing the city's ancient history * Ancient Reference Room — A library for ancient history resources * Craft Room * Atelier * High-Definition Theater * Audio-Visual Room * Café Corner * {{ill|Fujidera Municipal Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Municipal Library|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Municipal Library|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Municipal Library|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|13=User:Immanuelle/User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Municipal Library|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Municipal Library|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Municipal Library|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Municipal Library|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Municipal Library|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11622754}} Book Corner * Music Classrooms * Indoor Multi-Purpose Hall (rooftop with retractable roof) ==Major Exhibits== * "Waterfowl Haniwa," one of the largest and oldest in Japan — Designated as a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. * One of the largest "Boat-Shaped Haniwa" in Japan File:津堂城山古墳出土 形象埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Tsudo Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳|1=Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsudo Shiroyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|13=User:Immanuelle/User:Immanuelle/Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsudo Shiroyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsudo Shiroyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun|qq=}} excavated figurative haniwa
{{small|The three waterfowl-shaped haniwa in the foreground are National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}.|qq=}} File:岡古墳出土 埴輪 船.JPG|Boat-shaped haniwa from {{ill|Oka Kofun|ja|岡古墳|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oka Kofun|qq=}} File:井真成墓誌 (身, 複製).JPG|{{ill|Shinsei Sei|ja|井真成|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsei Sei|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsei Sei|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsei Sei|qq=}} Epitaph Main Section (Replica) File:井真成墓誌 (蓋, 複製).JPG|Jing Zhencheng Epitaph Lid Section (Replica) File:西墓山古墳 主体部.JPG|Main burial chamber of {{ill|Nishihayama Kofun|ja|西墓山古墳|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishihayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishihayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishihayama Kofun|qq=}} ==Access== * 8-minute walk from {{ill|Fujiidera Station|en|Fujiidera Station|qid=Q5507418|lt=Fujidera Station|1=Fujiidera Station}} on the [[:en:Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]] ==Nearby Attractions== * {{ill|Fujii-dera|en|Fujii-dera|qid=Q6406788|lt=Fujii-dera|1=Fujii-dera}} * {{ill|Yachū-ji|en|Yachū-ji|qid=Q11645008|lt=Yachū-ji|1=Yachū-ji}} * {{ill|Nonoue Hachiman Shrine|ja|野々上八幡神社|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nonoue Hachiman Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nonoue Hachiman Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nonoue Hachiman Shrine|1=Nonoue Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11644886}} * {{ill|Karakuni Shrine|ja|辛国神社|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuni Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuni Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuni Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|qid=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Cluster|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}} (World Cultural Heritage) ** Particularly the '''{{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamisanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamisanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|13=User:Immanuelle/User:Immanuelle/Okamisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamisanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamisanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamisanzai Kofun|qq=}}''' (Ega Nagano West Tomb), located immediately to the east ==See Also== {{Commonscat|Aiserushura-hall|qq=}} * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|en|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|qid=Q4557237|lt=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|1=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum}} (Shura sled exhibit) * {{ill|Osaka_Prefectural_Museum_of_Yayoi_Culture|en|Osaka_Prefectural_Museum_of_Yayoi_Culture|qid=Q11441080|lt=Osaka Prefectural Museum of Yayoi Culture|1=Osaka Prefectural Museum of Yayoi Culture}} * {{ill|Shinsei Sei|ja|井真成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsei Sei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11373728|1=I no Manari}} — After a temporary "homecoming" exhibit of the epitaph, replicas are now displayed ==References== {{Reflist|qq=}} * ==External Links== * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/shisetsuannaibasu/kyoikubunka/1387002133055.html AICEL Shura Hall (Fujidera Municipal Lifelong Learning Center)｜Fujidera City] (Accessed June 24, 2018) * [http://academic3.plala.or.jp/fujinan/fujisi/kakuchi/shisetsu/shi/syura/syuraholl.htm AICEL Shura Hall (Fujidera Municipal Lifelong Learning Center)｜Fujidera Minami Elementary School] (Accessed June 24, 2018) * [http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/shurahall.html AICEL Shura Hall (Fujidera Municipal Lifelong Learning Center)｜Fujidera Tourism Association] (Accessed June 24, 2018) {{translated page|ja|アイセルシュラホール|version=95034186|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{kofun navbar}} [[ja:アイセルシュラホール]] {{Commons category|Aiserushura-hall}} [[simple:AICEL Shura Hall]] {{wikidata link|Q11282212}}
